content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh

## Summary

1 actionable issue found; threshold gating for propose_new_bead overcounts final-review sources, potentially creating premature NDJSON proposals.

## Amendments

1. In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1064-1073` and `1459-1468`, `TerminalReviewFinding::from_final_review` converts a single final-review amendment into `occurrence_count = amendment.sources.len().max(1)`, and thresholding in `reconcile_proposed_bead_records` sums `occurrence_count` across findings. This makes one amendment with two sources count as 2 and cross the default threshold 2, causing a `proposed-beads.ndjson` record even though only one finding exists. The contract says to propose only when the same `proposed_bead_summary` appears in N or more findings (not sources), so this over-triggers at run time.
   Classification: fix_current_bead
   Rationale: Align counting to number of findings, not source cardinality, to preserve threshold semantics and reduce false-positive bead proposals.
